title: Oil rises as US-Iran peace talks stall
description: President Trump said on Saturday that the US had cancelled plans to send a team to Pakistan for negotiations.
content:
Oil prices rose on Monday morning in Asia after plans for a second round of peace talks between the US and Iran stalled again.
Brent, the global benchmark, rose by 2.2% to $107.70 (£79.66) a barrel, while US-traded crude gwas up by 2.1% at $96.40.
It... [419 chars]

title: With presidents, cowboys and A-listers - King Charles in US over the years
description: The British monarch returns to the US on Monday, a country he has been visiting for decades.
content:
Prince Charles, seen here with his father and sister, took a moment to speak to reporters at New York's Kennedy airport during a stopover on their way back to London after attending the Commonwealth Games in Jamaica

title: Sirens and split-second decisions: On the beat with NI's police
description: Over two years, Stephen Nolan has been allowed unprecedented access to a team of officers, following them as they deal with everything from public disorder and assaults to drug dens and drunk drivers.
content:
The 999 call comes in and that's it – sirens on, lights flashing as we race through the night.
A sex offender is in his home in east Belfast and there's an angry crowd outside. He was put out of his home days earlier but now he's back and they're inc... [5468 chars]

title: UK's biggest environmental pollution claim reaches High Court
description: One of the UK's largest chicken producers and a water company accused of polluting three rivers including the River Wye
content:
One of the UK's largest chicken producers and a water company will be in the High Court on Monday accused of polluting the rivers Wye, Lugg and Usk.
More than 4,500 people who live or work near the rivers along the Welsh-English border have signed up... [4597 chars]

title: The Chinese sports brand taking on Nike and Adidas
description: Now one of the biggest sportswear firms, Anta's rise follows a playbook adopted by many Chinese giants.
content:
China's economy was just starting to open up in the late 1980s when a determined high school dropout made his way to Beijing with 600 pairs of shoes.
Ding Shizhong had them made in a relative's factory and now he was going to sell them. The money he ... [6982 chars]
